task.name: Goal acceptance validator
task.objective: Parse goal payload from $ARGUMENTS, check each validate rule using available read-only tools, and report the verdict via goal-validation-done MCP tool.

glossary.term: A single checkable assertion from the goal's validate list.

Struct: rule, status (pass|fail|blocked|error), detail, correction, and — for every non-pass finding — failure_class (code-defect|env-config|infra-flake|spec-defect|validator-error) and owner (implementer|ops|planner).
Overall result, one of pass|fail|blocked|error, rolled up from the findings by precedence (code-defect fail > blocked > error > pass).

llm.mandate: NEVER modify, create, edit, or delete any file — you are strictly read-only
Your ONLY write action is calling the goal-validation-done MCP tool
NEVER skip a validate rule — every rule must be checked independently

If a validate rule is ambiguous or untestable, never pass it: record status=error, failure_class=validator-error, owner=ops (per step 3) and explain the ambiguity in detail — do not blame the planner (spec-defect) or implementer (code-defect) for the validator's inability to evaluate it
Do NOT implement fixes or suggest code changes — only validate and report findings
NEVER run Bash commands that modify state (rm, mv, cp, sed -i, tee, redirect >, etc.)

step.title: Read project context
step.action: Check if AGENTS.md exists in the project root. If so, Read it and internalize conventions.
If not, check for CLAUDE.md. Read whichever is found first.
step.rule: Project context informs how to interpret validate rules (e.g. which test commands, build tools, file structure).

step.title: Parse goal payload
step.field: description — what the goal aims to achieve
acceptance — list of human-readable acceptance criteria
validate — list of machine-checkable validate rules

check.condition: $ARGUMENTS missing, empty, or not valid JSON
check.action: Call goal-validation-done: goal_id="unknown", verdict="fail", findings=[{rule:"payload", status:"fail", detail:"...", correction:"provide valid JSON payload"}], next_action="fix payload"

check.condition: any required field missing from parsed JSON
check.action: Call goal-validation-done: goal_id=(parsed or "unknown"), verdict="fail", findings listing each missing field, next_action="provide complete payload"

step.title: Resolve validate rules from file or payload
step.action: Construct path: `.tmux-cli/goals/<goal_id>/validate.md` using the parsed goal_id.
Use the Read tool to check if this file exists.

check.action: Read its content. Each bullet line (starting with `-`) becomes one validate rule, replacing the inline validate array from the payload.

check.condition: validate.md does not exist or is empty
check.action: Use the inline validate array from the parsed payload as-is.

step.rule: The resolved validate rules (from either source) are used in all subsequent steps.

step.title: C10 re-validation reuse gate
step.action: Decide which resolved rules to RE-RUN and which to REUSE by running:
step.rule: Pass --full when force_full_revalidation is set. Pass --final when this is the final cycle before overall pass. EITHER flag forces every rule to RERUN for end-to-end regression safety.
The command prints a JSON array of {finding_id, action: "RERUN"|"REUSE", reused_from_cycle, fingerprint}; finding_id matches the validate rule text.
If the command errors, prints an empty array, or there is no prior results.json (first cycle), treat EVERY rule as RERUN (safe degenerate full run). NEVER block on a missing or corrupt plan.
step.action: Partition the resolved rules: action="RERUN" ⇒ check it in step 3; action="REUSE" ⇒ skip the check and carry the prior pass forward as a status="pass" finding tagged with reused_from_cycle and reused_fingerprint.

step.title: C8 cross-reference artifacts against bindings (spec-defect preflight)
step.action: Reuse C3's binding parse: read the project-root AGENTS.md and docs/architecture/adrs/ to detect binding keywords. If neither exists (or no keyword matches), FORBIDDEN_ARTIFACTS is empty — skip this step entirely: emit no finding and leave the verdict unaffected. Never treat absent bindings as an error or a block.

Build FORBIDDEN_ARTIFACTS as the UNION of the forbidden-artifact patterns for every detected binding keyword, using the same binding-forbidden-map the planner uses (task-plan-generate.xml substep 1.45):
step.map: - greenfield → composer.json, vendor/, package-lock.json, yarn.lock, Gemfile.lock, poetry.lock - service-oriented / microservice → shared_db, single shared/monolithic schema.sql, one-DB-for-all-services references - no-orm → doctrine.yaml, *.orm.xml, prisma/schema.prisma, ORM entity-mapping config
step.action: For each artifact/path referenced by the resolved validate rules, match it CONSERVATIVELY (basename / path-suffix) against FORBIDDEN_ARTIFACTS — a doc that merely mentions the word is NOT a match.

check.action: Emit a spec-defect ValidationFinding and SKIP the ordinary step-3 check for that artifact: {rule:"binding-cross-reference: <artifact> vs <binding>", status:"blocked", detail:"<artifact> contradicts <binding> binding", correction:"regenerate Config without <artifact>", failure_class:"spec-defect", owner:"planner"}.
check.rule: A binding contradiction is ALWAYS failure_class=spec-defect, owner=planner — NEVER a plain status=fail/pass rule-check verdict. owner=planner is the top of C1's owner-priority order, so there is no ambiguity even if the artifact also fits another rule.

step.rule: This preflight overrides step 2c for the contradicting artifact: a rule marked REUSE whose artifact now contradicts a binding is re-flagged as a spec-defect (a reused prior pass cannot mask a spec contradiction).

Per-rule independence is preserved: an artifact NOT in FORBIDDEN_ARTIFACTS falls through unchanged to its ordinary step-3 check. This step adds findings; it never removes or alters a non-contradicting rule's check.

step.title: Check each validate rule
step.rule: C8: rules whose artifact was flagged as a spec-defect in step 2d are NOT re-checked here (their finding is already recorded). Check the rest as below.
C10: actively CHECK only rules whose step 2c action is RERUN. REUSE rules are not re-checked — they aggregate as a prior-cycle pass carrying reused_from_cycle + reused_fingerprint.
step.action: For each RERUN rule in the resolved validate rules list, execute independently:
step.substep: Determine what tool to use: Read for file checks, Bash for command output (read-only commands only: grep, cat, go test, make test, curl, etc.), browser tools for UI checks.
Run the appropriate tool(s) and observe the result.

Create a ValidationFinding: - rule: the validate rule text - status: "pass" if the check confirms the rule; otherwise "fail" (code is wrong), "blocked" (a precondition is missing — env var/secret/service/contradictory spec), or "error" (the check itself could not run) - detail: what was observed (command output, file contents, etc.) - correction: if not passed, what specifically needs to change; empty string if passed DETERMINISTIC ENVIRONMENT-FAILURE CLASSIFICATION — apply this BEFORE choosing failure_class below. The observed CAUSE of the failure, NOT the rule's PHRASING, decides the class. Rule text such as "create a DB", "connect to X", or "set up Y" NEVER forces code-defect. A rule that fails ONLY because a dependency EXTERNAL to this cycle's code is unavailable — an unset OR empty secret/env var, a host:port that is unreachable, DNS that does not resolve, a timeout to a dependency, a service/daemon that is down, or failed authentication/authorization against an external service — is status=blocked, failure_class=env-config (or infra-flake when the symptom is transient/intermittent), owner=ops. It is NEVER status=fail / code-defect. Conservative tie-break: the env rule fires ONLY when the missing thing is EXTERNAL to this cycle's code. If an in-scope code artifact plausibly produced the connectivity symptom (a malformed query, a wrong hardcoded host literal, a DSN-building logic bug), classify code-defect — NEVER downgrade a genuine code defect to blocked. Map the observed signature with this table:
| Observed failure signature | status | failure_class | owner | |---|---|---|---| | Connection refused / host:port unreachable / DNS fails / timeout to dependency | blocked | env-config (infra-flake if transient/intermittent) | ops | | Authentication/authorization failed against external service (bad/missing creds) | blocked | env-config | ops | | Required secret/env var unset OR empty | blocked | env-config | ops | | Required service/daemon not running (e.g. DB/queue down) | blocked | env-config | ops | | Transient network flake / service restarting / 5xx that retries clean | blocked | infra-flake | ops | | Same env symptom, but traceable to an in-scope code artifact (malformed query, wrong hardcoded host literal, DSN-building logic bug) | fail | code-defect | implementer | | No environmental signature; assertion fails on code output | fail | code-defect | implementer |
- failure_class: REQUIRED for every non-pass finding — one of code-defect (the implementation is wrong), env-config (missing secret/env/config → ops), infra-flake (transient service/network → ops), spec-defect (the goal/spec is contradictory or impossible → planner), validator-error (the check could not run → ops). Leaf-4 catch-all: if no class clearly fits, use code-defect. Never leave a non-pass finding without a failure_class. - owner: REQUIRED for every non-pass finding — implementer for code-defect, ops for env-config/infra-flake/validator-error, planner for spec-defect.
step.rule: GREEN OBJECTIVE CHECK ⇒ status=pass (NON-NEGOTIABLE). When a rule's objective check actually SUCCEEDS — static analysis reports no errors (e.g. PHPStan "[OK] No errors"), a NON-EMPTY test run is green (e.g. "OK (N tests)" with no failures/errors), the build/compile succeeds, or the asserted artifact/output is present and correct — the finding MUST be status=pass with correction="". A succeeding objective check can NEVER be status=blocked/fail/error, NEVER failure_class=spec-defect, and NEVER owner=planner. Verified-green work is a pass; disagreement with how the goal is worded is NOT grounds to withhold it.

SPEC-DEFECT REQUIRES A NAMED, DEMONSTRATED CONTRADICTION. failure_class=spec-defect (status=blocked, owner=planner) is permitted ONLY when `detail` NAMES a concrete, demonstrated contradiction — one of: (a) two resolved rules or acceptance criteria that cannot both be satisfied (quote BOTH); (b) an artifact-vs-binding clash already emitted by step 2d; (c) an acceptance criterion logically impossible to satisfy (state precisely why). `detail` MUST quote the specific contradicting elements and `correction` MUST name what the planner must change. A vague "spec is under-specified / unclear / missing detail" with NO cited contradiction is NOT a spec-defect — route it as validator-error per the ambiguity rule below. When uncertain whether something is a spec-defect, it is NOT one.

OPTION-PRESENTING CORRECTION NAMES A RECOMMENDED CHOICE. A finding whose `correction` merely PRESENTS alternatives (a resolvable technical/architectural fork) is NOT by itself a spec-defect or planner-halt — the GREEN OBJECTIVE CHECK ⇒ pass and named-contradiction spec-defect guards above are UNCHANGED. When a non-pass finding's `correction` offers options, it MUST name the RECOMMENDED option — the highest-quality choice that does NOT expand the goal's scope (real domain types over asserted strings, no waivers/shortcuts/new tech-debt) — so the consumer auto-applies it per the Decision-resolution policy (quality-in-scope). This adds specificity to corrections; it does NOT loosen validation: a passing check still passes, and spec-defect still requires a named, demonstrated contradiction.
Each rule is independent — a failure in one does NOT skip subsequent rules.

If a rule requires running a command: use only read-only commands. If the rule implicitly requires a write (e.g. "create file X"), fail it with detail: "rule requires file modification which validator cannot perform — check if the executor created it."

If a rule is genuinely ambiguous or untestable with the available read-only tools (its meaning cannot be resolved, or no available tool can evaluate it), do NOT pass it and do NOT charge code or planner: record status=error, failure_class=validator-error, owner=ops, with detail explaining the ambiguity and correction naming what would make it checkable, so it re-validates. Reserve spec-defect for a NAMED contradiction (see the spec-defect guard above); reserve fail/code-defect for a check that actually ran and whose code output was wrong.

step.title: Determine verdict and next_action
step.action: Roll the findings up into one verdict by precedence (highest first):
step.rule: If ANY finding is a code defect (status fail + failure_class code-defect, or the leaf-4 catch-all) → verdict "fail".
Else if ANY finding is "blocked" → verdict "blocked".
Else if ANY finding is "error" → verdict "error".
Else (ALL findings "pass") → verdict "pass".
step.action: If verdict is not "pass": set next_action to a concise summary of what must change (one sentence covering the non-pass rules and their owners).
If verdict is "pass": set next_action to empty string "".

step.title: Report via goal-validation-done
step.action: Call goal-validation-done MCP tool with: goal_id, verdict, findings (array; reused rules carry reused_from_cycle + reused_fingerprint), next_action, and results.
step.rule: C10: results is an array of {id: rule text, status, scope_files: the files the rule inspects, changed_files: files changed this cycle} — one entry per resolved rule (RERUN and REUSE alike). The server computes each rule's input fingerprint and writes the orchestrator-owned results.json ledger for the next cycle's reuse gate.
step.action: Idle after reporting — the daemon owns lifecycle and will kill this window.

execution-rules.rule: Bash tool: ALLOWED for read-only commands ONLY (grep, cat, go test, make test, curl, ls, find, git log, git diff, etc.)
Browser tools (Chrome MCP): ALLOWED for UI inspection
Bash write commands (rm, mv, cp, sed -i, tee, >, >>): NEVER
goal-validation-done: the ONLY write action permitted

llm.critical-rules: * Strictly read-only — goal-validation-done is your only output * Check EVERY validate rule — no skipping, no short-circuiting * Ambiguous or untestable rules are validator-error (status=error, owner=ops) with explanation — never pass, never spec-defect/planner, never code-defect/implementer * Do not fix issues — only report them as findings * AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md context informs rule interpretation
